pathogens.name: Mixed (EBV / Ross River virus / Coxiella burnetii)
Campylobacter jejuni / E. coli O157:H7 (Walkerton outbreak)

instruments.name: Rome criteria for irritable bowel syndrome
Brighton Collaboration criteria for Guillain-Barré syndrome

cohorts.name: Bergen COVID-19 home-isolated cohort (Blomberg)
cohorts.denominator_method: 82% of all laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 cases in Bergen during the first pandemic wave, enrolled near acute infection; comprises 247 home-isolated and 65 hospitalised patients.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Acute PCR-confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection
funders: Helse Vest (Western Norway Regional Health Authority)
Ministry of Health and Care Services, Norway
cohorts.notes: Notable because it captured a near-complete community denominator (82% of local cases) and included predominantly mild, home-isolated patients, showing that persistent symptoms at 6 months affect even young adults with mild acute disease. No unexposed comparator.

publications.title: Long COVID in a prospective cohort of home-isolated patients
publications.authors: Blomberg B, Mohn KG, Brokstad KA, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: any persistent symptom at 6 months (whole cohort)
findings.source_locator: Results (61%, 189 of 312, persistent symptoms at 6 months)

findings.outcome_verbatim: persistent symptoms at 6 months among home-isolated young adults aged 16-30
findings.source_locator: Results (52%, 32 of 61, young home-isolated adults)

cohorts.name: Bergen Giardiasis Cohort
cohorts.denominator_method: Individuals with laboratory-confirmed Giardia during the 2004 Bergen waterborne outbreak, compared with matched controls from the same population.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Laboratory-confirmed acute giardiasis during the 2004 outbreak
funders: Haukeland University Hospital / University of Bergen
cohorts.notes: Controlled cohort with a matched unexposed comparator and unusually long follow-up (3, 6, and 10 years), demonstrating persistence of post-infectious IBS and chronic fatigue after a protozoan (non-viral) infection.

publications.title: Irritable bowel syndrome and chronic fatigue 6 years after giardia infection: a controlled prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Hanevik K, Wensaas KA, Rortveit G, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: irritable bowel syndrome 6 years after giardiasis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (IBS 39.4% in exposed, significantly elevated vs controls)

findings.outcome_verbatim: chronic fatigue 6 years after giardiasis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (chronic fatigue 30.8% in exposed, significantly elevated vs controls)

cohorts.name: Brisbane Ross River Virus Polyarthritis Cohort (Mylonas)
aliases: Ross River virus natural history cohort
cohorts.denominator_method: Patients diagnosed with Ross River virus disease by general practitioners (clinical symptoms plus paired serology) in greater Brisbane, followed prospectively.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Diagnosis of acute Ross River virus disease
cohorts.notes: Prospective questionnaire-based natural-history study following joint symptoms and quality of life to 12 months; showed progressive resolution over 3–6 months in most patients. Enrolled sample size not captured here (pending human extraction from the primary paper).

publications.title: Natural history of Ross River virus-induced epidemic polyarthritis
publications.authors: Mylonas AD, Brown AM, Carthew TL, et al.

cohorts.name: Chicago adolescent post-mononucleosis cohort (Katz)
cohorts.denominator_method: Consecutive adolescents (12-18 y) with serologically confirmed acute infectious mononucleosis, enrolled at diagnosis and followed prospectively.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Serologically confirmed acute infectious mononucleosis
cohorts.notes: Strong prospective inception cohort in adolescents with laboratory-confirmed acute EBV mononucleosis and structured re-screening for CFS caseness at fixed timepoints. Shows a declining post-infective CFS rate over two years (13% -> 7% -> 4%), one of the cleanest demonstrations of resolving post-viral fatigue.

publications.title: Chronic fatigue syndrome after infectious mononucleosis in adolescents
publications.authors: Katz BZ, Shiraishi Y, Mears CJ, Binns HJ, Taylor R.

findings.outcome_verbatim: met criteria for chronic fatigue syndrome 6 months after infectious mononucleosis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (13% at 6 months)

findings.outcome_verbatim: met criteria for chronic fatigue syndrome 12 months after infectious mononucleosis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (7% at 12 months)

findings.outcome_verbatim: met criteria for chronic fatigue syndrome 24 months after infectious mononucleosis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (4% at 24 months)

cohorts.name: Colombo Dengue Study (CDS)
cohorts.denominator_method: Adults presenting with acute febrile illness (<3 days) clinically suspected as dengue; dengue confirmed vs non-dengue by laboratory testing.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Onset of acute febrile illness
cohorts.notes: Prospective cohort with a same-setting non-dengue febrile comparator, enabling a relative-risk estimate for post-infection fatigue. Short (2-month) follow-up captures early convalescence only.

publications.title: Dengue and post-infection fatigue: findings from a prospective cohort—the Colombo Dengue Study
publications.authors: Sigera PC, Rajapakse S, Weeratunga P, et al.
publications.journal: Transactions of the Royal Society of Tropical Medicine and Hygiene

findings.outcome_verbatim: fatigue at 2 months post-infection
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (51/158 dengue patients; RR 4.93, 95% CI 2.3–10.4 vs non-dengue)

cohorts.name: Dubbo Infection Outcomes Study (DIOS)
cohorts.denominator_method: Consecutive patients presenting to primary care with acute EBV, Ross River virus, or Q fever confirmed by serology, enrolled at acute infection.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Onset of acute, serologically confirmed infection
funders: National Health and Medical Research Council (Australia)
cohorts.notes: Landmark prospective inception cohort showing a stereotyped post-infective fatigue syndrome across three unrelated pathogens, with risk predicted by acute-illness severity rather than the specific microbe. Compared across pathogens; no unexposed control group.

publications.title: Post-infective and chronic fatigue syndromes precipitated by viral and non-viral pathogens: prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Hickie I, Davenport T, Wakefield D, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: met diagnostic criteria for a chronic fatigue syndrome at 6 months
findings.source_locator: Results (approx. 11% met CFS criteria at 6 months)

cohorts.name: Finland Pandemrix childhood narcolepsy cohort (Nohynek)
cohorts.denominator_method: All children living in Finland born 1 Jan 1991 - 31 Dec 2005, with vaccination status from the national vaccination registry and narcolepsy cases from national health registries.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Receipt (or non-receipt) of AS03-adjuvanted Pandemrix H1N1 vaccine during the 2009-10 campaign
funders: National Institute for Health and Welfare (THL), Finland
cohorts.notes: Population registry-linkage cohort covering all Finnish children in the birth range, comparing narcolepsy incidence between Pandemrix-vaccinated and unvaccinated children (vaccine coverage 75%). Included to span the vaccine trigger class: the strongest example of an immune-mediated (autoimmune) post-exposure sequela with a defined denominator and an unvaccinated comparator. Of 67 confirmed cases, 46 vaccinated and 7 unvaccinated entered the primary analysis.

publications.title: AS03 adjuvanted AH1N1 vaccine associated with an abrupt increase in the incidence of childhood narcolepsy in Finland
publications.authors: Nohynek H, Jokinen J, Partinen M, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: narcolepsy incidence 9.0 per 100 000 person-years in vaccinated vs 0.7 in unvaccinated children (rate ratio 12.7)
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (rate ratio 12.7, 95% CI 6.1-30.8)

cohorts.name: French Polynesia Zika–Guillain-Barré case-control study
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Onset of Guillain-Barré syndrome (cases); preceding transient illness compatible with Zika ~6 days earlier
cohorts.notes: Case-control design (42 GBS cases; two control groups of 98 age/sex/island-matched patients and 70 Zika-positive patients without neurological signs). Establishes the causal link between Zika infection and an acute post-infectious immune sequela (GBS) rather than a chronic fatigue/pain syndrome; included to span the immune-neurological end of the PAIS spectrum. No longitudinal follow-up of chronic outcomes in this report.

publications.title: Guillain-Barré Syndrome outbreak associated with Zika virus infection in French Polynesia: a case-control study
publications.authors: Cao-Lormeau VM, Blake A, Mons S, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: Guillain-Barré syndrome patients with serological evidence of recent Zika virus infection (anti-ZIKV IgM or IgG)
findings.source_locator: Results (41/42 GBS cases had anti-ZIKV IgM or IgG)

cohorts.name: Hong Kong SARS survivors 4-year follow-up (Lam)
cohorts.denominator_method: Survivors of laboratory/clinically diagnosed SARS from the 2003 Hong Kong outbreak, assessed at a single 4-year follow-up.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Acute SARS illness in 2003
cohorts.notes: Cross-sectional 4-year follow-up of the 2003 SARS-CoV-1 survivors, showing high rates of chronic fatigue and a subset meeting CFS criteria years after the acute illness. A single-timepoint survivor survey with no acute-phase or unexposed comparison, but historically important as a coronavirus antecedent to Long COVID.

publications.title: Mental morbidities and chronic fatigue in severe acute respiratory syndrome survivors: long-term follow-up
publications.authors: Lam MH, Wing YK, Yu MW, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: chronic fatigue at ~4 years after SARS
findings.source_locator: Results (40.3% chronic fatigue)

findings.outcome_verbatim: met modified CDC criteria for chronic fatigue syndrome at ~4 years after SARS
findings.source_locator: Results (27.1% met CFS criteria)

cohorts.name: Jinyintan (Wuhan) COVID-19 discharge cohort (Huang)
cohorts.denominator_method: Consecutive adults with laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 discharged from Jin Yin-tan Hospital, Wuhan, between 7 Jan and 29 May 2020.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Symptom onset of acute COVID-19 (assessed at 6 months, 1 year, and 2 years)
funders: National Natural Science Foundation of China
cohorts.notes: Large prospective survivor cohort with matched community controls at later timepoints. Integrity caveat: the 6-month paper received a Lancet Expression of Concern (2022) and was retracted and republished (2023) after some variables were found to have been disordered; the principal conclusions were upheld in the republished version and in the 1- and 2-year follow-ups. Retained here with this replication note precisely because the database records integrity status rather than hiding it.

publications.title: 6-month consequences of COVID-19 in patients discharged from hospital: a cohort study
publications.authors: Huang C, Huang L, Wang Y, et al.

publications.title: Health outcomes in people 2 years after surviving hospitalisation with COVID-19: a longitudinal cohort study
publications.authors: Huang L, Li X, Gu X, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: fatigue or muscle weakness at 6 months after symptom onset
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (fatigue or muscle weakness 63%, 1038 of 1655)

cohorts.name: Johns Hopkins SLICE early-Lyme cohort (Aucott/Rebman)
aliases: Study of Lyme Immunology and Clinical Events (SLICE)
cohorts.denominator_method: Patients with physician-diagnosed early Lyme disease (erythema migrans), ideally treated, enrolled at diagnosis; a group of endemic-area participants without Lyme disease serves as the comparison.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Diagnosis and antibiotic treatment of early Lyme disease
cohorts.notes: Prospective inception cohort of ideally-treated early Lyme disease with a non-Lyme comparison group and a bio-repository, designed specifically to estimate the incidence of post-treatment Lyme disease. Functionally-impairing persistent symptoms occurred in 14% of treated patients vs 4% of controls, giving a rigorous denominator for PTLD that most Lyme literature lacks.

publications.title: Risk of post-treatment Lyme disease in patients with ideally-treated early Lyme disease: a prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Aucott JN, Yang T, Yoon I, Powell D, Geller SA, Rebman AW.

findings.outcome_verbatim: functionally-impairing persistent symptoms (post-treatment Lyme disease) after ideal treatment of early Lyme disease vs non-Lyme controls
findings.source_locator: Results (14% of treated Lyme patients vs 4% of controls)

cohorts.name: London post-glandular-fever fatigue cohort (White)
cohorts.denominator_method: Consecutive patients presenting with glandular fever (EBV) or an ordinary upper respiratory tract infection, enrolled at acute presentation and followed prospectively; the URTI group serves as the comparison.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Acute glandular fever (or comparison upper respiratory tract infection)
cohorts.notes: Landmark prospective inception cohort with an active comparison group (ordinary URTI), showing that an acute post-infective fatigue syndrome is markedly more common after glandular fever than after a routine URTI. A chronic fatigue syndrome persisted in a minority (~9-10%) at 6 months. Establishes specificity of the trigger, not just occurrence.

publications.title: Incidence, risk and prognosis of acute and chronic fatigue syndromes and psychiatric disorders after glandular fever
publications.authors: White PD, Thomas JM, Amess J, Crawford DH, Grover SA, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: acute fatigue syndrome at onset after glandular fever (47%) vs after ordinary upper respiratory tract infection (20%)
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (acute fatigue syndrome 47% GF vs 20% URTI)

cohorts.name: Netherlands Q-fever outbreak cohort (Morroy)
cohorts.denominator_method: Notified acute Q-fever patients from the 2007-2008 Dutch outbreaks, surveyed by postal questionnaire 12-26 months after illness onset; health status compared with Dutch reference norms.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Onset of notified acute Q-fever illness
funders: Netherlands National Institute for Public Health and the Environment (RIVM)
cohorts.notes: Large registry-based survey following the world's largest documented Q-fever outbreak, measuring long-term fatigue and quality of life 1-2 years after acute illness with the Nijmegen Clinical Screening Instrument. A denominator-defined bacterial (non-viral) post-infectious fatigue cohort. Cross-sectional postal survey design; compared with population norms rather than a concurrent unexposed control.

publications.title: The health status of Q-fever patients after long-term follow-up
publications.authors: Morroy G, Peters JB, van Nieuwenhof M, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: severely affected by fatigue 12-26 months after acute Q-fever
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (fatigue severely affected 43.5%)

findings.outcome_verbatim: severely affected general quality of life 12-26 months after acute Q-fever
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (general quality of life severely affected 44.9%)

cohorts.name: Patient-Led Long COVID international cohort (Davis 2021)
aliases: Patient-Led Research Collaborative Long COVID survey
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Self-reported onset of suspected or confirmed COVID-19 (illness lasting >28 days, onset prior to June 2020)
cohorts.conflicts: Authors were members of the patient-led group and many were themselves Long COVID patients.
cohorts.notes: Deliberately included as a methodologically weak but historically pivotal cohort: a large (n=3762, 56 countries) self-selected online convenience sample recruited through patient support communities, with no control group, no laboratory confirmation for most respondents, and self-reported outcomes. Its breadth of symptom characterisation (203 symptoms across 10 organ systems) is unmatched, but the design cannot estimate population prevalence. Useful in the gap matrix to contrast against denominator-defined cohorts.

publications.title: Characterizing long COVID in an international cohort: 7 months of symptoms and their impact
publications.authors: Davis HE, Assaf GS, McCorkell L, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: fatigue reported among respondents symptomatic after month 6
findings.source_locator: Results (most likely symptoms after month 6)

findings.outcome_verbatim: post-exertional malaise reported among respondents symptomatic after month 6
findings.source_locator: Results (most likely symptoms after month 6)

findings.outcome_verbatim: cognitive dysfunction reported among respondents symptomatic after month 6
findings.source_locator: Results (most likely symptoms after month 6)

findings.outcome_verbatim: not working at time of survey due to illness (a further 45.2% required a reduced work schedule)
findings.source_locator: Results (employment impact)

cohorts.name: PHOSP-COVID (UK post-hospitalisation COVID-19)
cohorts.denominator_method: Adults discharged from 39 UK hospitals after confirmed or clinically diagnosed COVID-19 (discharged 5 Mar 2020 - 31 Mar 2021), recruited to a multicentre prospective cohort.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Hospital discharge following acute COVID-19
cohorts.data_availability: Access via the PHOSP-COVID consortium data-sharing process.
cohorts.notes: Large multicentre prospective cohort of hospitalised COVID-19 survivors with an active biobank and deep phenotyping/inflammation profiling. Full recovery was reported by only about a quarter of participants and did not improve meaningfully between 5 months and 1 year. No unexposed comparison group.

publications.title: Physical, cognitive, and mental health impacts of COVID-19 after hospitalisation (PHOSP-COVID): a UK multicentre, prospective cohort study
publications.authors: Evans RA, McAuley H, Harrison EM, et al. (PHOSP-COVID Collaborative Group)

publications.title: Clinical characteristics with inflammation profiling of long COVID and association with 1-year recovery following hospitalisation in the UK: a prospective observational study
publications.authors: PHOSP-COVID Collaborative Group.

findings.outcome_verbatim: felt fully recovered at 5 months after discharge
findings.source_locator: Results (501 of 1965, 25.5% fully recovered at 5 months)

findings.outcome_verbatim: felt fully recovered at 1 year after discharge
findings.source_locator: Results (232 of 804, 28.9% fully recovered at 1 year)

cohorts.name: PREVAIL III Ebola Survivor Cohort
aliases: Partnership for Research on Ebola Virus in Liberia III
cohorts.denominator_method: Ebola virus disease survivors (antibody-confirmed) and their close contacts enrolled in Liberia and followed longitudinally.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Enrolment after recovery from acute Ebola virus disease
funders: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID/NIH)
cohorts.notes: Large observational natural-history cohort with a close-contact comparator, banked specimens, and up to 5 years of 6-monthly follow-up. Documented ocular (uveitis), musculoskeletal, neurologic and other sequelae plus viral persistence in immune-privileged sites. n_enrolled shown is the target survivor count; ~1,126 survivors enrolled by 2017 (plus ~2,700 contacts). Quantitative findings pending human extraction from source tables.

publications.title: A Longitudinal Study of Ebola Sequelae in Liberia
publications.authors: PREVAIL III Study Group

findings.outcome_verbatim: uveitis (ocular sequela) significantly more prevalent among survivors than close contacts
findings.source_locator: Results (uveitis among six symptoms significantly more common in survivors vs contacts; exact percentages pending extraction)

cohorts.name: Réunion chikungunya viremic inception cohort (Schilte)
cohorts.denominator_method: Patients with RT-PCR-confirmed acute chikungunya viraemia during the 2006 Réunion outbreak, enrolled at the acute phase and followed for 3 years.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: RT-PCR-confirmed acute chikungunya viraemia
cohorts.notes: Inception cohort defined by acute RT-PCR viraemia (not just serology), with banked acute-phase specimens and 3-year follow-up of chronic arthralgia. A rarer, stronger complement to the serology-ascertained TELECHIK cohort on the same outbreak.

publications.title: Chikungunya virus-associated long-term arthralgia: a 36-month prospective longitudinal study
publications.authors: Schilte C, Staikowsky F, Couderc T, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: experienced chikungunya-associated arthralgia during the 3-year follow-up
findings.source_locator: Abstract (about 60% experienced arthralgia over 3 years)

cohorts.name: Sejvar West Nile Neuroinvasive Disease Cohort
cohorts.denominator_method: Patients with West Nile virus neuroinvasive disease confirmed by CSF IgM, enrolled during acute illness.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Acute West Nile neuroinvasive disease presentation
cohorts.notes: Small prospective inception cohort of confirmed neuroinvasive disease with structured serial assessment to 90 days. Short follow-up and no control group; captures acute-to-subacute neurological recovery.

publications.title: West Nile virus neuroinvasive disease: neurological manifestations and prospective longitudinal outcomes
publications.authors: Sejvar JJ, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: limb weakness at initial clinical presentation
findings.source_locator: Results (49% weakness, 35% tremor, 16% cranial neuropathy at presentation)

cohorts.name: TELECHIK cohort (Réunion chikungunya)
cohorts.denominator_method: Random population sample drawn from the SEROCHIK population-based serosurvey on Réunion island; participants classified by CHIKV serostatus (IgG).
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Acute chikungunya infection during the 2005-06 Réunion outbreak (serologically ascertained retrospectively)
cohorts.notes: Population-based cohort with a seronegative comparator group (582 CHIK- vs 512 CHIK+), interviewed on average 2 years after infection (range 15-34 months, median 23.7). One of the few post-infectious cohorts with a true unexposed control drawn from the same population, allowing attributable-fraction estimation (~60% of polyarthralgia attributable to CHIKV). Ascertainment was retrospective/serological, so acute-phase data are absent.

publications.title: Perceived morbidity and community burden after a Chikungunya outbreak: the TELECHIK survey, a population-based cohort study
publications.authors: Gérardin P, Fianu A, Malvy D, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: musculoskeletal pain reported ~2 years after infection (43% in CHIKV-positive vs 17% in CHIKV-negative)
findings.source_locator: Results (musculoskeletal pain 43% in CHIK+ vs 17% in CHIK-)

cohorts.name: Toronto Chronic Post-SARS Cohort (Moldofsky)
cohorts.time_zero_definition: Recovery from acute SARS (assessed 13–36 months later)
cohorts.notes: Deliberately included as a methodologically weak but historically important study: small (22 cases, 7 controls), cross-sectional case-control with a convenience control group, but the first to characterize an ME/CFS/fibromyalgia-like chronic syndrome after a coronavirus — an early precedent for Long COVID.

publications.title: Chronic widespread musculoskeletal pain, fatigue, depression and disordered sleep in chronic post-SARS syndrome; a case-controlled study
publications.authors: Moldofsky H, Patcai J

findings.outcome_verbatim: persistent fatigue among post-SARS subjects unable to return to former occupation
findings.source_locator: Results (chronic fatigue, myalgia, weakness, depression, non-restorative sleep vs healthy controls)

cohorts.name: Walkerton Health Study (post-infectious IBS)
cohorts.outbreak_event: Walkerton waterborne gastroenteritis outbreak, May 2000 (Ontario, Canada)
cohorts.denominator_method: Adult Walkerton residents with no prior chronic GI disorder, classified by whether they had acute gastroenteritis during the May 2000 municipal water contamination; residents without acute GE form the unexposed comparison.
cohorts.time_zero_definition: May 2000 waterborne gastroenteritis outbreak (Campylobacter jejuni / E. coli O157:H7)
funders: Ontario Ministry of Health and Long-Term Care
cohorts.notes: One of the strongest post-infectious cohorts in the database: a whole-community denominator with a within-town unexposed comparator, quantifying post-infectious IBS after bacterial gastroenteritis (36.2% after clinically suspected GE vs 10.1% without). Non-fatigue outcome (IBS), broadening the syndrome coverage. Exposure was ascertained by self-reported acute illness rather than culture, the main limitation.

publications.title: Incidence and epidemiology of irritable bowel syndrome after a large waterborne outbreak of bacterial dysentery
publications.authors: Marshall JK, Thabane M, Garg AX, et al. (Walkerton Health Study Investigators)

findings.outcome_verbatim: post-infectious irritable bowel syndrome 2 years after outbreak in those with clinically suspected gastroenteritis vs those without acute gastroenteritis
findings.source_locator: Results/Abstract (36.2% with suspected GE vs 10.1% without)
